name: Impossibles (Chinese: 不可能浪点; Indonesian: Impossibles / Pantai Impossibles)
distance_from_dps_airport_km: Approximately 18-20 km from Ngurah Rai (DPS) Airport (around 40-50 min by car)
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~75-90 min by scooter; From Uluwatu: ~10-15 min by scooter
wave_type: Left-hand reef break / point break with multiple sections (Top, Middle, End / Uppers and Lowers); fast walls and barrels
skill_level: Advanced to Pro (not suitable for beginners; intermediates can ride smaller days with caution)
wave_height_range: 3-10+ ft; lines up best at 5 ft and above; holds well into double-overhead range
bottom_type: Reef (coral), deeper in sections but with shallow reef fingers on the inside
best_season: Dry season, May to October (peak: June-September); offshore SE trades and clean SW groundswells
best_tide: Mid to low tide for the lower/end section; mid to high tide for the first/top peaks; full low can expose shallow reef
best_wind: Offshore from East to Southeast (SE trade winds)
swell_consistency_rating: 5/5 during dry season (one of the most consistent breaks in Bali) [uncertain]
wave_length_meters: Up to ~400 m on a single section; over 1 km total reef length can link Top to End / into Bingin on rare large days
wave_speed_rating: Fast (very fast down-the-line sections; aptly named 'Impossibles' due to unmakeable speed)
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate to high; drop is not huge but the wave runs away quickly, demanding early commitment and high-line trim
secondary_peaks: Yes - Top (near Padang Padang / Villa Pemutih), Middle, and End (near Bingin); commonly called Uppers and Lowers
peak_crowd_level: 3/5 - less crowded than Uluwatu/Padang/Bingin due to longer paddle; spreads out across the 1 km reef; busiest 7-10am dry season
access: Multiple options: (1) paddle from Padang Padang beach (north end), (2) descend stairs at Bingin and paddle north along the reef, (3) Anantara Uluwatu Resort elevator/path down the cliff to mid-reef [uncertain on public access via Anantara]
paddle_out_method: Easiest via the channel at Padang Padang then drift north up the reef; alternatively keyhole through the inside reef channel on smaller days; from Bingin, paddle across shallow reef and lagoon with currents
entry_fee: Free for the surf break itself; Padang Padang beach parking ~IDR 5,000-10,000; Bingin beach parking ~IDR 5,000-10,000 and beach access fee ~IDR 10,000 [uncertain]
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-8:00 am) for glassy conditions; late afternoon if SE winds remain offshore
best_time_of_day: Pre-8 am to avoid Padang Padang crowds drifting up; sunset sessions also less busy
nearby_warung: Warungs and cafes at Padang Padang beach (Mango Tree, Padang Padang Surf Camp warung) and Bingin (Lucky Fish, Kelly's Warung, The Cashew Tree); Anantara resort F&B options
alternative_spot_within_10min: Padang Padang (when smaller and high tide), Bingin (best mid tide), Uluwatu (handles bigger swells), Dreamland (more forgiving beach break)
combo_trip_potential: N/A (Bali domestic spot; not typically part of boat-charter circuits)
hazards: Sharp shallow reef on the inside especially at low tide, fast unmakeable sections, strong currents along the reef, occasional sea urchins, reef fingers at exit
marine_hazards: Sea urchins (bulu babi) on inside reef, occasional jellyfish in wet season transition, rare stonefish in lagoons; treat reef cuts immediately with vinegar/iodine and antibiotics
reef_cut_severity_index: Medium-high - deeper than Uluwatu but inside reef fingers are sharp; reef rash and lacerations common when wiping out near exit
rip_current_pattern: Channel current between Padang Padang reef and Impossibles reef drifts surfers northward toward Bingin; outgoing tide accelerates this. Use it to your advantage on paddle out but be aware on return
lineup_etiquette: Spread out across long reef so rotation is informal; respect priority to surfer deepest/closest to peak; don't paddle around or snake locals; smile and say hi
localism_intensity: Low to medium - generally friendly due to space available; can get edgy on perfect crowded days but rarely aggressive
respect_protocol: Wait your turn, do not drop in, acknowledge locals, avoid hassling solo surfers on inside; keep beach/cliff areas clean
resident_local_legends: No single iconic Impossibles local equivalent to Mega Semadhi at Ulu; many Bukit pros (Made Adi Putra, Garut Widiarta, Rio Waida) surf here regularly [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Nearby Uluwatu Temple (Pura Luhur Uluwatu) - dress respectfully on cliffs; observe Nyepi (Day of Silence, March) - no surfing or beach access; menstruating women traditionally avoid temple visits
medical_nearby: Unicare Clinic Pecatu (24/7, surf injury specialists), BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua, Siloam Hospital Denpasar
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu ~3-4 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~12 km; Siloam Denpasar ~20 km
rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated jet ski rescue; informal help from local surfers and Anantara hotel staff [uncertain]
emergency_contact: Indonesia emergency: 112; Bali Search and Rescue (Basarnas): +62 361 9351111; Unicare Pecatu: +62 361 8470770 [uncertain]
nearest_surf_school: Padang Padang Surf Camp (intermediate-advanced coaching), Rip Curl School of Surf (Dreamland), Odyssey Surf School - though Impossibles itself is not a learner spot
board_rental_availability: Available at Padang Padang and Bingin beach warungs and nearby surf shops (Drifter Uluwatu, Single Fin shop, board rental stalls)
surf_photographer_available: Yes - freelance land/water photographers regularly shoot from the cliff and channel; especially active during contest season
photographer_recommendation: Local photographers post on Instagram (search #impossiblesbali); typical pricing IDR 200,000-500,000 per session set [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Anantara Uluwatu Resort (luxury cliff-side), Padang Padang Surf Camp, Bingin Garden, Mu Bali, Mick's Place, Temple Lodge, numerous villas in Pecatu
webcam_url: https://balicams.com/camera/impossibles ; https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/impossibles/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d42 [uncertain - Surfline access may require subscription]
drone_allowed: Generally tolerated for personal use; commercial drone use requires permits; avoid flying over crowded lineups and Anantara resort airspace [uncertain]
coworking_nearby: Outpost Ulu (Uluwatu), Tropical Nomad satellite, Drifter Uluwatu cafe with wifi; ~10-15 min ride away
avg_lesson_price: Not a beginner spot - lessons typically at Dreamland or Balangan; intermediate guiding ~IDR 600,000-1,200,000 per session [uncertain]
board_rental_daily: IDR 100,000-200,000 per day depending on board type and shop
water_quality_rating: Good in dry season (clear blue water); declines slightly in wet season due to runoff from cliffs and lagoon outflow; generally cleaner than Kuta/Canggu
annual_competition_window: No major standalone Impossibles event; sometimes overflow heats from Padang Padang Cup (Rip Curl Cup, typically July-August window) [uncertain]
community_events: Informal sunset sessions; nearby Single Fin Sunday at Single Fin Uluwatu is the main weekly gathering
